name: Hidden Memories of The Gardens Between

reviews.review_description: Absolutely beautiful mixed reality experience — the fog, lighting, and passthrough blend feel magical. Stunning visuals, music, and animations throughout. The puzzles are engaging and rewarding, specially collecting items, with a great sense of discovery. The hand tracking takes a bit of learning, but it’s great to see titles exploring this. The attention to detail and overall polish make it truly impressive.

developer_response.body: Yay! We loved reading your review. Thank you! Thanks for playing.

reviews.review_title: A fun and relaxing game for all ages
reviews.review_description: Hidden Memories consists of a set of puzzles where the player has to move time back and forth in order to change events and reach the end of the course. In this VR game, players can choose how they want to interact with the gameplay—play with an overview of the map, as if they were the god of the map, or have a more realistic and close-up view of the characters, as if they were walking the path alongside their two friends. This allows players to immerse themselves in the gameplay in the best way possible for a fantastic experience. In my opinion, I much preferred playing this game with an overview of the map, as this allowed me to quickly notice all the changes that happened to the map as I moved forward or backward in time. The puzzles are generally quite easy to complete. There is an increase in difficulty between levels, but nothing too difficult or impossible, which is why I believe this game is ideal for all ages. In addition to being a relaxing game that helps stimulate thinking, this game also offers an extra mini-game where the player has to find hidden items. For me, this was the highlight of the gameplay. I loved the way the items were hidden in the various maps; each map had a creative way of getting the player to acquire the various hidden items. As good as the game is, it also has some flaws. I didn't like that it wasn't explained to the player right from the start of the mini-game, which meant I had to repeat the first puzzles, as I wanted to collect all the hidden items. This is something that can be easily fixed. In addition, at some points in the gameplay, there are flashes of lights between levels and also in the cutscenes that are a little annoying. I would suggest reducing the brightness of these flashes or using a color that is less annoying to the player's eyes. Overall, for anyone thinking about buying the game, I 100% recommend it. This game is a very relaxing and fun immersive experience and I am loving it.

developer_response.body:
Thank you so much No0bTezz. Clearly, you're no noob at all ;)

We're glad you enjoyed the options you're presented with in this game, and how you can approach it you're own way. It's been interesting to hear from players of each camp about which they prefer; up-close or the overview. We're dividied within the team too and look forward to more players sharing their thoughts!

Heads-up about the flashing. Pleased to say that yes, that is totally fixed and we've never had a complaint since.

You're right that this game is designed for all ages, and also all languages, abilities and genders. Our goal has been since the start to make an approachable, intuitive game that has no fail states, no pressure, and in fact no words or text - instead telling the universal story of childhood through imagery and context, letting the character's reactions and the objects that are meaningful to them tell the story.

So excited to hear your favourite part is the hidden objects - that's what's new and we just spent that last two years building! It's the always awesome to hear what you just worked on is what player's love the most ;)

reviews.review_description:
Don't be deceived by the low(er) quality animation and simplicity of the first couple stages. The third stage layers on more difficulty, but never enough to be frustrating or needing days to think about (yet). As far as the hidden pieces, they aren't as easy to find as the simple environment would suggest. Some had me chuckling out loud with how cleverly they were hidden. If I had to compare to existing games, the titles Along Together (for the 2-character cooperative aspect), Spark of Light (for the lantern lighting opening different sections), and Curious Case of Stolen Pets (for the diorama style and hidden pieces) come to mind. 

My only fear is how short it might be (again, I'm not finished yet). Good deal on sale if it is as short as I think it will be.

developer_response.body: Thank you so much for your review. It brings a huge smile to hear that you're chuckling about how some items were hidden - it's no doubt we had fun making it. I hope you find the length to your satisfaction. Adding depth and breadth to the experience was one of our main goals with this game, as the original title was linear and had little for the player to explore and find more in it. We hope you continue to enjoy what we've added :)

reviews.review_title: A game with heart and soul
reviews.review_description: Another top-notch game with a diorama look that's worth checking out: Hidden Memories of the Garden Between. The story is about friendship between two children and their small surreal adventures. The gameplay involves puzzles and searching for things. The puzzles require some thought, rushing isn't an option. You can even use mixed reality if you like. Or you can swap the diorama perspective for a close-up or in-the-middle perspective.

developer_response.body: Great overview of the experience. We should have got you to write our store copy :)

reviews.review_title: An awesome and visually stunning Story
reviews.review_description:
Give it a try! :D
I went into this game not really sure how it would translate into VR. But then when you see how the levels, the camera angles and the time manipulation and puzzles work... WOW! This truly is an amazing 3D experiance. Lots of cool secrets, map and camera manipulation, time control and so much more! I really ended up loving this game and the style of it so much more than expected! :D 
Well worth it in my opinion, wonderful story, wonderfully made game and so much more to it than I initially expected. It translates into VR and 3D space so well <3 Great work! ^^

developer_response.body: Thank you so much. It means the world to see players responding like this. 🥺❤️

reviews.review_description:
This game offered a very interesting puzzle-oriented game. The puzzles ranged from acessible to difficult, and introduced many different mechanics through the story to create challenging scenarios. Nonetheless, it never felt like it was overly complicated or poorly designed, but rather making you think about the possibilities and the need to interact with the core mechanics.

Speaking of which - the core mechanics revolves around forwarding and pushing time backwards. It is an interesting way to promote the interaction with the game, and I feel like it creates an enthralling way to progress through the levels. Each of them told a different story within the memory book, that you could also exploit through a fun extra minigame within a game to find the many different items around the game.

The acessibility, controls and the overall playability of the game was sound. You had total freedom to manipulate around the world, and alter your point of view and you had a choice between a full world view or a closer, personal view, depending on your choice of immersion.

The only downside I could list as of right now would be the abundance of bright white flashes when you'd transition that unfortunately could strain your eyes a bit, but it is fixable by lowering the brightness - or timing these and closing your eyes.

Overall, a solid, great game,  at a very affordable price that can give you a few hours of solid entertainment - suitable for all ages!

Disregard the negative reviews. The art style and mechanics is pretty unique with pause, forward and rewind to solve puzzles. There is no voice acting so you have to figure out what’s going on through a lot of observation. It is cartoon like but it works well for the genre of storytelling and puzzles. 

Great job developers appreciate how you able to pull out tiny objects and paste into the comic books

Definítely recommend

developer_response.body: Thank you so much! Our illustrator was so touched to read your review.

reviews.review_title: Cool concept with a lot of concessions
reviews.review_description:
The very fact that this is available on the Quest 2 suggests that concessions were made. The resolution is way too low. Regarding the foviated rendering, the foviated area has less clarity than the eyeline resolution in Red Matter.

The graphics are fine, but it's clear that something more could have been done after seeing Ghost Town in action. 

The concept is actually really cool. It wouldn't keep me busy all day, but it's inventive and neat in the same way that A Fisherman's Tale is, if only less cerebral.

I'm not sure if it gets more complex. I hope so, but at the onset it's pretty easy.

There's an issue where the hands snap onto any object you grab, but the action duplicates your hands in VR, so any time you're holding a book - for instance - there are 4 hands in view. It's janky and weird.

Cool game, cool concept, and the animation is pretty decent. The plot is a little undefined. Even on the download page it's a single line of words that don't really add up to much of a story.

It's cool, but it needs work.

i play my fair share of puzzle games and this is one of my favorites in a while and especially the way the puzzles are played out. With moving time and using two different characters works so well. Some puzzles really stumped me and took time to figure them out. Along with finding different items and being able to play sat down, MR and go up close in VR its a great game to just sit and relax.

It's a decently rare opportunity to come across a VR game that I want to put my time into. VR is simply not a medium I give a whole lot of mind to, so for something to get my approval is often a big deal (perhaps biased and egotistical though haha). In regards to my impressions of Hidden Memories, most— if not all— are positive. 

Hidden Memories is focused on a very "toy box" type of experience—  where the player is put in a position of both watching and being the guiding hand to the lives of our main characters. Most puzzles follow a rather direct set of solutions, allowing the levels to require enough thinking to feel clever— while also providing enough trial and error to get players through with brute force. This is definitely less so the further the game goes on, with the puzzles requiring a lot more attention, but I think by that point the player is already familiar with the puzzle design and should feel right in their element.

Narratively the game is also rather simple— with the player having different means of interacting with the world around our main characters. There's even a subtle "I Spy" treasure hunt system, where players are encouraged to isolate certain collectibles that always have a habit of hiding right under your nose. There's really something for everyone here— be it the relaxing aesthetic, solving puzzles or interacting with the environment to find collectibles

If I had to really muster any gripe with the game— I might say that some of the controls are a little odd? Though most of the hiccups I've run into seem par for the course regarding VR, and I am simply not experienced with the platform as others are. By the end I was more than comfortable with how the control scheme functioned. Frankly I am not even sure if adjustments haven't already been made to the more "clunky" aspects of the game, so frankly my last qualm with it could have already been patched out.

Genuinely worth the time and purchase if you're aiming for a relaxing puzzle game and are already familiar with the VR format. It was a pleasure to get to play!
Very cleverly made. Gets your brain working.  👍

developer_response.body: Thank you A Dragon In VR. So pleased to hear you've enjoyed the puzzles! We had a great time crafting unique moments with puzzles that take you deeper into their memories. We loved hearing from you. Thanks!

reviews.review_description: 2 friends share cherished childhood memories, their takes go back and forth, winding and rewinding like cogs in a clock, puzzling the player until unstuck, rewarded with chords struck, visual delight and a poem it is
